channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Erling Haaland's Hometown Celebrates His Rise to Football Stardom
item.description: The people of Bryne, Norway, are celebrating Erling Haaland's meteoric rise to global football stardom, with the striker's hometown becoming a focal point for national pride ahead of the World Cup qua...

item.title: NBA Summer League Highlights: Dybantsa and Peterson Shine in Las Vegas Debut
item.description: The NBA summer league in Las Vegas provided a platform for top draft picks to showcase their skills. Washington Wizards' first overall pick, AJ Dybantsa, delivered a standout performance with 27 point...

item.title: Port Adelaide's Zak Butters and Mitch Georgiades suffer major injuries in AFL clash
item.description: In a brutal AFL match, Port Adelaide's star players Zak Butters and Mitch Georgiades sustained severe injuries that could end their seasons. Butters suffered a syndesmosis ankle injury after collapsin...

item.title: Linda Mwananchi Cancels Ol Kalou Rally Amid Alleged State Plot to Postpone By-Election
item.description: The Linda Mwananchi faction has pulled out of its planned July 12 rally in Ol Kalou, shifting to Nyahururu Town. The decision comes after alleging a coordinated effort by the IEBC and the ruling admin...

item.title: Matt Payne claims victory as De Pasquale stages remarkable comeback in Townsville 500
item.description: Matt Payne has solidified his position as the Supercars leader after dominating the second race of the Townsville 500, securing his third win of the season by a staggering 15.2885 seconds. The Grove R...

item.title: Zambales Provincial Gov't Reaffirms Sovereignty Over Scarborough Shoal
item.description: The Zambales provincial government has reaffirmed its territorial claim over the disputed Scarborough Shoal (Bajo de Masinloc), emphasizing the strategic and economic significance of the area. The rea...

item.title: D.C. Sees Record Tourism Despite Heat Wave, Boosting Economy
item.description: Washington, D.C., experienced a surge in tourism during the Fourth of July celebrations in 2026, despite facing an intense heat wave. The event drew hundreds of thousands of visitors, with hotel occup...

item.title: Toronto's Plan to Enhance Pedestrian-Friendly Streets
item.description: Toronto City Hall is advancing initiatives to create more pedestrian-friendly streets, aiming to improve urban mobility and safety. The article explores potential designs for these streets, such as wi...

item.title: Heatwave Continues Across UK Despite Local Cooling This Weekend
item.description: The UK is experiencing an ongoing heatwave with temperatures exceeding 35°C in parts of England, Wales, and Scotland. While some areas will see cooler conditions this weekend due to a strengthening no...

item.title: PMD Warns of Glacial Lake Outburst Floods in GB and KP Due to Westerly Wave
item.description: The Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD) has issued a critical alert for glacial lake outburst floods (Glofs) in Gilgit-Baltistan and Khyber Pakhtunkhwa due to an approaching fresh westerly wave. ...

item.title: Belgium's Penalty Appeal Rejected in Handball Incident
item.description: During a crucial match between Belgium and Spain, Belgian players protested a referee's decision to deny a penalty kick after a handball incident involving Manchester City's Rodri. The incident occurr...

item.title: Understanding Cord Blood Donations and Their Role in Medical Treatments
item.description: The article explains the process of cord blood donations, which involve collecting blood from the umbilical cord after childbirth. This blood is rich in hematopoietic stem cells that can be used to tr...

item.title: North Bay police investigate vandalism at new waterfront site amid possible hate crime concerns
item.description: North Bay police are investigating a potential hate or bias-motivated incident following vandalism at the newly opened Waterfront Canada Place. The incident occurred shortly after the venue's opening,...

item.title: South African Coaches Lead Tanzanian Clubs in NBC Premier League Rivalry
item.description: The NBC Premier League in Tanzania is set for an intense tactical rivalry as South African coaches Manqoba Mngqithi and Steve Barker take charge of Young Africans (Yanga) and Simba respectively. Mngqi...

item.title: House Prosecutors to Present More Witnesses in VP Sara Impeachment Trial
item.description: The House prosecution panel is intensifying its case against Vice President Sara Duterte by adding more witnesses as the impeachment trial enters its second week. Prosecutors Leila de Lima and Chel Di...

item.title: Kenya Railways Revives Nairobi-Nyahururu Train Service
item.description: Kenya Railways has confirmed the continued operation of the Nairobi-Gilgil-Olkalou-Nyahururu train, which resumed services after a 46-year hiatus. The revival aims to boost freight movement, cut trans...

item.title: Vogue's 1977 Star Wars Fashion Shoot: A Surreal Blend of Sci-Fi and Luxury
item.description: In November 1977, the US Vogue magazine featured a groundbreaking photo shoot that merged the worlds of Star Wars and high fashion. Photographer Eisuke Ishimuro and stylist Jade Hobson collaborated wi...

item.title: Italian Beach Clubs Face Disputes Over Packed Lunch Policies
item.description: Customers at private Italian beach clubs are clashing with management over policies banning packed lunches, sparking debates about affordability and hospitality. At Il Tirreno in Lazio, patrons like B...

item.title: KUCCPS and KMTC Warn Against Fake Admission Scams
item.description: KUCCPS and KMTC have issued warnings to students and applicants about scams related to fake admission letters and fraudulent activities. The Kenya University and College Central Placement Service (KUC...

item.title: Government allows private sector to manage foot-and-mouth disease vaccination efforts
item.description: The South African government has agreed to permit private entities to import, distribute, and administer foot-and-mouth disease (FMD) vaccines, following pressure from farmer groups and legal action. ...
